Category: Public technology company (visual discovery / social media / shopping platform)
Overview of the Company: Pinterest, Inc. operates a visual discovery and inspiration platform where users search, save ("pin"), and shop ideas across categories like fashion, home, recipes, and more. It emphasizes positive, aspirational content with strong e-commerce integration and AR/shopping tools. Monetized primarily through advertising (performance and brand), it serves 619M monthly active users with a focus on AI enhancements for personalized discovery and shopping. The company is profitable and cash-generative while investing in AI to drive Gen Z growth and advertising efficiency.
Company Headquarters: 651 Brannan Street, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA
Countries Operating In: United States; Canada; United Kingdom; Germany; France; Brazil; Mexico; Japan; Australia; India
Office Locations: Chicago, IL; New York, NY; Toronto, CA; London, UK; Berlin, DE; Paris, FR; Tokyo, JP; Sao Paulo, BR
Employee Size: 5,265 (FY 2026; up from 4,666 in 2025; post-layoffs adjustment from ~5,200 pre-restructuring)
Vision: To bring everyone the inspiration to create a life they love.
Mission: To bring everyone the inspiration to create a life they love.
Values: Put Pinners First; Aim for Extraordinary; Create Belonging; Act as One; Win or Learn
Interesting Facts: Pinterest was originally a shopping app called "Tote"; Over 80 billion monthly searches are performed on the platform as of 2026.
Recent News: Feb 2026: FY 2025 revenue $4.222B (+16%), net income $417M, MAUs 619M (+12%); Jan 2026: Layoffs of <15% workforce (~600-700 roles) and office reductions to fund AI pivot; New Chief Business Officer Lee Brown and Chief Marketing Officer Claudine Cheever appointed; Feb 2026: Kecia Steelman (Ulta Beauty CEO) added to Board
Regulatory & Compliance Status: GDPR Compliant; CCPA Compliant; SEC Registered; COPPA Compliant
Legal Issues / Controversies: Shareholder litigation regarding workplace culture (Resolved); Ongoing compliance with EU Digital Services Act

Macro Risks: Advertising market cyclicality; Consumer spend shifts; AI-search disruption of traditional SEO

Customer testimonial: Pinterest is where our customers go to plan, and now where they go to buy. - CMO, IKEA

Pain Points Being Addressed: Choice paralysis in shopping; Lack of creative inspiration; Intrusive/negative social media environments; High friction between discovery and purchase
